task_completion.description: The agent located and reviewed the project brief on the desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A document viewer, PDF reader, or text editor is open displaying a project brief that contains lighting requirements.

task_completion.description: The agent modeled at least 5 light fixtures inside the building.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender 3D viewport shows the FZK-Haus building model with at least 5 new objects (e.g., meshes representing lights) placed inside. The Blender Outliner panel should also list these new objects.

task_completion.description: The modeled fixtures are classified specifically as IfcLightFixture entities.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Bonsai/BlenderBIM Object Properties panel, the 'IFC Class' or 'Object Type' for the selected fixture(s) is explicitly set to 'IfcLightFixture'.

task_completion.description: The fixtures are explicitly assigned to a building storey for proper spatial containment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Outliner (configured to show the IFC spatial tree) shows the light fixtures nested under an IfcBuildingStorey (e.g., Ground Floor, Upper Floor), or the spatial containment section in the Object Properties shows the assignment.

task_completion.description: A property set containing at least one specific lighting domain property ('Wattage', 'LuminousFlux', 'Voltage', or 'LightColor') is created and assigned to the fixtures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Object Properties panel under IFC Object Properties shows a custom Property Set assigned to the fixture, containing at least one of the required keys ('Wattage', 'LuminousFlux', 'Voltage', or 'LightColor') with a value.

task_completion.description: The enriched IFC project is saved to /home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_lighting.ifc.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender's 'Save Project' or 'Export IFC' dialog shows the file path '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_lighting.ifc', or a file manager/terminal window confirms the existence of this file in the specified directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Bonsai (BlenderBIM) GUI to model and classify the fixtures.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the Blender interface (3D viewport, Outliner, Properties panels) rather than just a terminal window running a Python script (e.g., using IfcOpenShell directly) to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The property sets were created and assigned using the Bonsai interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Bonsai property set creation UI is visible in the screenshots, showing the manual entry or selection of properties like 'Wattage' or 'LuminousFlux'.

integrity.description: The agent saved the file through Blender/Bonsai's native save/export functionality.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Blender interface shows the 'Export IFC' or 'Save Project' dialog being used, rather than a terminal command simply copying or renaming an existing file to match the output name.
